title: Using SELECT first
summary: Learn how and why to use SELECT statements to preview affected rows before running UPDATE or DELETE commands, ensuring safer data modifications in the inventory_items table.

sketchBlocks.title: Why Preview with SELECT?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Before making changes to your data with UPDATE or DELETE, it's a best practice to first run a SELECT query with the same WHERE condition. This lets you see exactly which rows will be affected, helping you avoid accidental changes or deletions. For example:

```sql
-- Preview which items are marked as test items
SELECT id, name, is_test FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

If the preview looks correct, you can then safely proceed with your UPDATE or DELETE statement using the same WHERE clause.

sketchBlocks.title: Safe UPDATE Workflow
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you want to change the status of all test items from 'draft' to 'inactive'. First, preview the affected rows:

```sql
SELECT id, name, status FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1 AND status = 'draft';
```

If the results match your expectations, then run:

```sql
UPDATE inventory_items SET status = 'inactive' WHERE is_test = 1 AND status = 'draft';
```

This two-step process helps prevent accidental updates.

sketchBlocks.title: Safe DELETE Workflow
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Deleting data is irreversible, so always preview first! For example, to remove all test items:

```sql
SELECT id, name FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

After confirming the preview, you can run:

```sql
DELETE FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

This habit protects you from deleting the wrong rows.

quizDraft.title: Preview rows before updating
quizDraft.prompt: You want to update the status to 'inactive' for all inventory items in the 'Accessories' category that are currently 'draft'. Write a SELECT statement to preview which rows would be affected.
quizDraft.hint: Use SELECT to show id, name, and status for rows where category is 'Accessories' and status is 'draft'.

help.concept: Previewing with SELECT helps you see which rows match your intended criteria before making changes.
help.hint_1: Write a SELECT query with the same WHERE condition you would use in the UPDATE.
help.hint_2: Select id, name, and status from inventory_items where category is 'Accessories' and status is 'draft'.

quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT id, name, status FROM inventory_items WHERE category = 'Accessories' AND status = 'draft';

quizDraft.title: Preview rows before deleting
quizDraft.prompt: Before deleting all test items from the inventory, write a SELECT statement to preview which rows will be deleted.
quizDraft.hint: Select id and name for rows where is_test equals 1.

help.concept: Always preview with SELECT before running a DELETE to avoid removing unintended data.
help.hint_1: Use SELECT with the same WHERE clause you would use in the DELETE.
help.hint_2: Select id and name from inventory_items where is_test is 1.

quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT id, name FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;

quizDraft.title: Update after preview
quizDraft.prompt: After previewing, update the status to 'inactive' for all inventory items in the 'Accessories' category that are currently 'draft'. Write the UPDATE statement.
quizDraft.hint: Use UPDATE with SET and the same WHERE condition you previewed.

help.concept: Once you confirm the preview, use the same WHERE clause in your UPDATE to safely modify only the intended rows.
help.hint_1: UPDATE the inventory_items table, set status to 'inactive' where category is 'Accessories' and status is 'draft'.
help.hint_2: Use UPDATE inventory_items SET status = 'inactive' WHERE category = 'Accessories' AND status = 'draft';

quizDraft.solutionCode: UPDATE inventory_items SET status = 'inactive' WHERE category = 'Accessories' AND status = 'draft';

quizDraft.title: Why use SELECT before UPDATE or DELETE?
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are reasons to run a SELECT statement before executing an UPDATE or DELETE? (Choose all that apply.)
quizDraft.hint: Think about safety, accuracy, and preventing mistakes.

help.concept: Running a SELECT first helps you verify which rows will be affected and avoid accidental data changes.
help.hint_1: Previewing helps you confirm your WHERE clause and avoid mistakes.
help.hint_2: SELECT lets you see the affected rows, catch errors, and prevent unwanted changes.

options.a: To confirm which rows will be affected.
options.b: To avoid accidentally updating or deleting the wrong data.
options.c: To speed up the UPDATE or DELETE operation.
options.d: To catch mistakes in your WHERE clause.
options.e: Because SELECT always makes changes to the data.

quizDraft.title: Purpose of SELECT preview
quizDraft.prompt: Fill in the blank: Before running an UPDATE or DELETE, running a SELECT with the same WHERE clause helps you ____ which rows will be affected.
quizDraft.hint: What does the SELECT statement show you?

help.concept: A SELECT preview lets you check which rows match your criteria before making changes.
help.hint_1: It helps you check or confirm the affected rows.
help.hint_2: The SELECT lets you see the rows that would be changed or deleted.

quizDraft.template: Before running an UPDATE or DELETE, running a SELECT with the same WHERE clause helps you ____ which rows will be affected.
